questions.text: Write the number that comes after 19.
How many days are there in one week?
Riya has 5 red balls and 3 green balls. How many balls does she have in all? Show how you count.
questions.answer: Riya has 5 + 3 = 8 balls in all. Count: start at 5, then add 3 more (6, 7, 8).

questions.text: Name any two shapes you see around you and write one thing about each.
questions.answer: Example: A clock is a circle — it is round with no corners. A book is a rectangle — it has 4 sides and 4 corners.

questions.text:
Assertion (A): 10 is greater than 7.
Reason (R): When we count, 10 comes after 7.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: 10 comes after 7 in counting, so 10 is greater — R correctly explains A.

questions.text:
Assertion (A): A triangle has 3 corners.
Reason (R): A triangle has 3 sides.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners; having 3 sides makes 3 corners. Both true and related.

questions.text: Match the number with its name.
Match the object with its shape.
questions.explanation: Ball-Circle, Book-Rectangle, Pizza slice-Triangle, Dice face-Square.
Adding zero does not change the number. True.
10 is greater than 4, so this is False.
